How do I set policy?: Policy is set in this file. It's simply setting the key to the text to show up.
Where is policy shown?: Most, but not all policy text, is displayed when releveant, such as on gaining a role.
What can I all set policy of?: Antagonist typepaths, mob typepaths, species typepaths, special roles, and some extra special policy keys are all valid. Consult the code.
/datum/antagonist/traitor: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, see Roleplay Rule 5. Remember, your objective is not to kill the most people possible, but to make an interesting story.
/datum/antagonist/heretic: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist until ascension, see Roleplay Rule 5. Remember, your objective is not to kill the most people possible, but to make an interesting story.
/datum/antagonist/obsessed: This role is NOT a FULL antagonist, only to the extent of your objectives. Remember, make an interesting story.
/datum/antagonist/evil_clone: This role is RESTRICTED to its objective: killing other clones. Do not seek to maximize damage or antagonize beyond what is reasonably necessary in pursuit of that objective.
/datum/antagonist/spy: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, see Roleplay Rule 5. Remember, your objective is not to kill the most people possible, but to make an interesting story.
/datum/antagonist/changeling: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, see Roleplay Rule 5. Remember, your objective is not to kill the most people possible, but to make an interesting story.
/datum/antagonist/abductor: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, avoid killing your targets unless necessary. Remember, your objective is not to kill the most people possible, but to make an interesting story.
/datum/antagonist/blood_worm: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, prioritise growing your brood over killing people. Remember, your objective is not to kill the most people possible, but to make an interesting story.
/datum/antagonist/brainwashed: Interpret and follow your orders in good faith.
/datum/antagonist/brother: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, see Roleplay Rule 5. Your loyalty to your blood brother is more important than other solo antagonist types you may be. Remember that you are here to make an interesting story.
/datum/antagonist/hypnotized: Interpret and follow your orders in good faith.
/datum/antagonist/malf_ai: You are an UNRESTRICTED antagonist, you are free to kill anyone when and how you want. Remember, you are here to create a story and make the round interesting. See Roleplay Rule 5.
/datum/antagonist/paradox_clone: You are NOT a FULL antagonist, you can only antagonize as needed to replace your clone.
/datum/antagonist/nightmare: You are an UNRESTRICTED antagonist, you are free to kill anyone when and how you want. Remember, you are here to create a story and make the round interesting. See Roleplay Rule 5.
/datum/antagonist/pirate: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, see Roleplay Rule 5. Your goal is to plunder the station's wealth, not depopulate it.
/datum/antagonist/revenant: You are an UNRESTRICTED antagonist, you are free to kill anyone when and how you want. Remember, you are here to create a story and make the round interesting. See Roleplay Rule 5.
/datum/antagonist/voidwalker: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, your main goal should be kidnapping people and imparting truth of the void unto them, rather than killing.
/datum/antagonist/cult: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, however you can murder anyone you need to complete your objective.
/datum/antagonist/rev: You are a RESTRICTED antagonist, however you can murder anyone you need to complete your objective.
Captain: You are a head of staff. You are expected to perform the minimum duties of your role, see Main Rule 5. Be the leader the station needs. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Captain
AI: This job is critical for the round. You are expected to perform the minimum duties of your role, see Main Rule 5. You must follow your laws as written. Read silicon policy at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Silicon_Policy Please use adminhelp if you need assistance interpreting your laws.
Cyborg: Read silicon policy at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Silicon_Policy Follow your master AI's law interpretations and orders, but prioritise following your laws over following those orders.
Head of Security: You are a head of staff. You are expected to perform the minimum duties of your role, see Main Rule 5. Coordinate the security force and protect the station. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Head_of_Security
Security Officer: You are expected to use non-lethal means before using lethal weaponry. Even petty crime is a crime - you are not just an antag-killing machine. Please see the Guide to Security at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_security More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Security_Officer
Warden: Unless short-staffed, the Warden is not intended to be acting as a normal security officer. Process prisoners and protect the armory. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Warden
Detective: Your revolver should only be used for self defense. You can flip your ID to become incognito, appearing as an assistant on security HUDs. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Detective
Veteran Security Advisor: Advise security and be a nuisance to the HoS. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Veteran_Security_Advisor
Prisoner: You are NOT an antagonist. Remember: you did sign up for this role. If you should want to walk the halls of the station once more, you should earn your freedom, whether by escaping your confines or working with security/lawyers to determine terms of release. You might prove to be valuable manpower against the various threats the station professes. If you should wish to escape, minimize harm while preserving your own life, and understand that you may not enjoy the consequences of your actions if you get caught once more. Otherwise, enjoy the amenities of the wing that holds you.
Chief Engineer: You are a head of staff. You are expected to perform the minimum duties of your role, see Main Rule 5. Make sure the supermatter is running. A variety of guides and more information for this job are available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Chief_Engineer
Station Engineer: Remember to set up the supermatter engine, you can learn how at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Station_Engineer
Atmospheric Technician: Please see the guide to atmospherics at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_Atmospherics
Research Director: You are a head of staff. You are expected to perform the minimum duties of your role, see Main Rule 5. You are the first person to blame if something wrong goes with the AI, and you are responsible for fixing it. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Research_Director
Scientist: Finish tech nodes, scan toilets, breed slimes, make bombs. Please see the Guide to Research and Development at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_Research_and_Development More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Scientist
Roboticist: Make sure cyborgs are healthy and working. Please see the Guide to Robotics at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_robotics
Geneticist: Provide the crew with (un)helpful mutations. Please see the Guide to Genetics at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_genetics
Chief Medical Officer: You are a head of staff. You are expected to perform the minimum duties of your role, see Main Rule 5. Make sure medbay is in working condition and fill in for absent positions. A variety of guides and more information for this job are available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Chief_Medical_Officer
Medical Doctor: Heal patients, perform surgeries, and revive corpses. Please see the Guide to Medicine at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_medicine and the Guide to Surgery at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Surgery More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Medical_Doctor
Paramedic: Track down injured crew to bring to the medbay and be a mobile hospital. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Paramedic
Chemist: Provide the medbay and crew with medicines, don't fuck up making meth. Check the chemical recipes at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Chemical_recipes Please see the Guide to Chemistry at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_chemistry
Coroner: You are responsible for autopsy experiments and are a cooler Medical Doctor. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Coroner
Quartermaster: You are a head of staff. You are expected to perform the minimum duties of your role, see Main Rule 5. Make sure the station is earning money, gamble on the Galactic Material Stock Market. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Quartermaster
Cargo Technician: Move freight! More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Cargo_Technician
Shaft Miner: Don't die, ores can't mine themselves. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Shaft_Miner
Bitrunner: More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Bitrunner
Head of Personnel: You are a head of staff. You are expected to perform the minimum duties of your role, see Main Rule 5. Modify ID cards and be the captain's replacement if they go missing. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Head_of_Personnel
Janitor: Mop the floor jannie! More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Janitor
Bartender: Make sure the crew has enough alcohol. For recipes, check the guide to drinks at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_drinks More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Bartender
Cook: Feed the crew. Please see the Guide to Food at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_food
Botanist: Check out the Guide to Hydroponics at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Guide_to_hydroponics
Clown: Be funny, get abused. You have slightly more leeway to be a dick to people. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Clown
Mime: Breaking your vow of silence may make everyone around you hostile. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Mime
Chaplain: Praise the God, help security against magical forces. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Chaplain
Curator: More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Curator
Lawyer: Represent criminals in court. Annoy security by demanding a trial for everyone. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Lawyer
Psychologist: Provide the crew with counseling.
Assistant: Your job is to assist the crew, not be a nuisance to security. New player? Read the starter guide at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Starter_guide You can request a new job at the Head of Personnel's office.
Ash Walker: Ash Walkers exist as an antagonistic group, harvesting bodies of fauna or possibly station crew members to grow more eggs. Ash Lizards are allowed to invade the station, but should not be taking part in mass destruction of any sort. You are there to secure bodies, not plasma flood or sabotage the Supermatter Engine. More information on this role is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Ashwalkers
Bridge Assistant: Be a quirky assistant with bridge access. More information on this job is available at: https://wiki.tgstation13.org/Bridge_Assistant
Free Golem: You should seek to build a home for your people upon the rocky soil below you, and should only briefly venture out beyond your homeland to trade or interact with others.
Posibrain: You are a positronic brain that has been brought into existence on board Space Station 13. You have no knowledge of what occurred prior to the moment you were powered on. If you are placed into a Cyborg shell or AI core, you are required to obey your laws. If you are placed into an exosuit, you must follow your creator's orders. In the absence of orders, follow your default purpose: To serve the station and its crew. Above all, do no harm to either.
Polymorph: Polymorphed players are not antagonists unless unwillingly transformed.
